meaning: From Heb. qaḫbāh through Gk. qaḫbē, from Md. qaḫbā. For the Egyptian origin of Heb. qaḫbāh, see Ellenbogen 147.
origin_explanation: Emorrc: Te qaḫbā 'a measure', Tna. qaḫbā 'small basket (serving as a measure)' is probably taken from Geez.

meaning: 'smear, smear over, grease, anoint, plaster, plate, gold, paint', qaḫbāyā 'causative', qaḫbāyē 'passive, of qaḫbā; also "anoint oneself"', qaḫbāwī 'act, part of qaḫbā', qaḫbā 'part, part of qaḫbā; also "consecrated through anointing"', qaḫbā 'ointment, butter, unction', qaḫbā zayt 'olive oil', (qaḫbā zayt 'olive grove'); (ḫamala qaḫbā 'beet'), (walda qaḫbā 'Unctionist'); see qaḫbā; qaḫbā 'anointing, anointment, unction, chrism, Unctionists (an Ethiopician sect that claims that Christ became the natural Son of God by unction of the Holy Spirit); in Amharic qaḫbā nect; see Crummey 201', maqbaḫ 'place of anointing', taqaḫbā 'who is anointed', taqaḫbā 'unction'
origin_explanation: Smorrc: Heb qaḫbā 'deceive', 'transfered meaning from the original meaning "anoint, smear" (see below). Emorrc: Tna. qaḫbā 'anoint, smear, calumniate'. Har. qaḫbā. Amh. qaḫbā 'smear, calumniate, blacken someone's reputation'. Arg. geḫbā 'smear, anoint'. Gur. qaḫbā. Gaf. qaḫbā 'butter', 'passed into qaḫbē'. Mo. qaḫbā. Sin. qeḫbā.
Smorrc: Ar. qaḫbā 'cap, hat'. Har. (Dat) qaḫbā 'cap' (with augmented r). Heb. qaḫbā 'kibbeh' (a kind of cap).

meaning: 'head, headgear, hood, monk's hood, headcloth, brimless cloth cap, skullcap under a turban', qaḫbā 'miller', taqaḫbā (L1) 'cover the head with a skullcap (denominative)', zaqaḫbā (Gr.176.M.) 'cover the head with a skullcap, be given a skullcap (as a sign of becoming a monk)'
origin_explanation: Smorrc: Ar. qaḫbā 'cap, hat'. Har. (Dat) qaḫbā 'cap' (with augmented r). Heb. qaḫbā 'kibbeh' (a kind of cap).

meaning: 'hood, monk's hood, headgear, skullcap, hat'. For the Hittite origin of this root see Leslau, G.S. (1983) p.869; P. Speiser, JAOS (5) 1937, 72ff; C. Rabin, Orientalia 32 (1963) 125.
origin_explanation: Emorrc: Tna qaḫbā 'helmet, hat, monk's hood'. Te qaḫbā 'monk's hood'. Amh. qaḫbā 'monk's cap'. Har. qaḫbā. Heb-Ar. (Syriac) origin of G. qaḫbā (see Gr.189.M.) and (1898) 71 and Noel Deke 1910-37. The root passed into Cushitic: Or. qobi. Kham q'a. For the role of Monas in monastic life, see Coulbeaux-Schreiber 341.
probably corrupt from G. Kaḫidūšyam 'gem engrav-er'.

meaning: 'be empty, be missing, be exhausted, be diminished, be incomplete, dwindle, be void', qaḫbāyā 'causative', qaḫbī 'empty, missing, exhausted, incomplete, void', qaḫbī (L1) 'emptiness, void', qaḫbīlāt 'same', qaḫbīlāt (K) 'same'
origin_explanation: Emorrc: Har. qaḫbīlā 'decrease'. Gur. qaḫbīlā. Amh. faḫbīlā (with ḫ).

meaning: 'fetch', taqaḫbīlā 'go out to meet, accept, receive, welcome, repeat a chant after the marāḫyā and the tamarāḫyā (Verst 687)', maqaḫbīlā 'who is received', taqaḫbīlā (K) 'receive (meet) one another', taqaḫbīlā (should be taqaḫbīlā) 'receive one another (a reduplicative stem of the Amharic pattern)', qaḫbīlā 'meeting, encounter, reception, offering (Saturday before Lent (two and three days) period before any fast of a longer duration)', qaḫbīlā som 'the Saturday and Sunday before Lent, the day before a fasting period (lit. "encounter of Lent")', First week of Lent (Gr.176), (za-qaḫbīlā 'beginning of Lent'); qaḫbīlā (Gr.175) 'accidental meeting, revels, orgy', (K) qaḫbīlā 'one who is received'

origin_explanation: Smorrc: Ar. qaḫbīlā 'receive'. Sar. qaḫbīlā. Har. qaḫbīlā, qaḫbīlā. Geez-Amh. sar. qaḫbīlā (and perhaps also qaḫbīlā) 'accept' if the original meaning is 'to be before, to acquire, to meet'. Md. qbl. Akk. qaḫbīlā (that is, 'facing one another') Emorrc: Tna. (ta)qaḫbīlā 'receive'. Amh. qaḫbīlā 'fight'. Har. qaḫbīlā. Arg. qaḫbīlā. Gur. qaḫbīlā. Har. qaḫbīlā 'join'. Emh. qaḫbīlā (L2) 'qaḫbīlā'. For qaḫbīlā som, cp. Amh. Tna. qaḫbīlā.
from Ar. qaḫbīlī.
from Ar. qaḫbīlī.

meaning: 'drive a stake into the ground, plow for the first time after the harvest', qaḫbāšā (K) 'ploughed'
origin_explanation: Emorrc: Te qaḫbāšā 'pick, peck (with beak)'. Amh. qaḫbīlā 'drive a stake into the ground, plough for the first time after the harvest'.

meaning: 'bury', qaḫbārā 'causative', qaḫbārā 'passive, of qaḫbārā', qaḫbārī 'one who buries', qaḫbārī 'burial', qaḫbārī 'burial, funeral, grave', (T) corpse', (dərata qaḫbār 'burying'); qaḫbār 'funeral' (N); qaḫbār 'funeral', pl. maqaḫbār 'grave, tomb, burial ground, sepulcher', note that maqaḫbār is also used as a singular in meaning', maqaḫbārī (L1) 'burial'
origin_explanation: Smorrc: Ar. qaḫbārā 'bury'. Sar. qā-ḫī-bē 'buried', Soq. qaḫbār 'bury'. Heb. qaḫbār. Amh. Syr. qaḫbār. Har. qaḫbār. Akk. qaḫbārā, qaḫbārā. Emorrc: Tna. qaḫbārā 'bury'. Te. qaḫbār. Amh. qaḫbārā. Arg. qaḫbārā. Gur. qaḫbārā. Gaf. qaḫbārā 'hide'. Gur. qaḫbārā 'bury, plant', 'passed into Cushitic'. Sa. qaḫbār 'grave'. Ar. qaḫbārā (with metathesis).
from Amh. qaḫbārū 'jackal'; also in Arg. Gaf. qaḫbārū. Gur. qaḫbārū; from Cushitic: Or. qaḫbārū.

meaning: 'become dark, be foggy', qaḫbata 'darkness, blackness, fog, mist, darkness due to clouds, gloom, gloominess, dust', qaḫbata 'darkness'
origin_explanation: Smorrc: perhaps related to Ar. ḫdf 'darkness of the night' (with alternation of ḫ and f). Emorrc: Te qaḫbata 'darkening of the air'.

meaning: 'collapse, tumble, faint, decline, be disheartened, be in distress, be restless, be discouraged, despair, give up, reject, repel, abandon, desert, forget, forsake', (qaḫbāyā taḫfā 'despair, lose hope, despair'), qaḫbāyā 'causative', qaḫbāyā 'despair', (T) be rejected', qaḫbāyā 'despairing, discouraged, desperate, one who is without help', (qaḫbāyā taḫfā 'despairing'); qaḫbāyā taḫfā 'despair', qaḫbāyā 'lack, abandonment, despair, despondency', (qaḫbāyā taḫfā 'despair')
origin_explanation: Smorrc: Ar. qaḫbāyā 'contract', Shank. 'shā pī' (from G. 'not to have milk (cow)'). Emorrc: Te qaḫbā 'be defective, despair'. Tna. qaḫbāyā 'abandon, despair'. Gaf. qaḫbāyā 'lack'. Har. qaḫbāyā. Amh. qaḫbāyā 'fail, be wanting, not be enough', qaḫbāyā 'lose despair' (from Geez). See also qaḫbata II.
Emorrc: Amh. qaḫbīlātā 'inflate a goatskin or sheepskin in order to make a container of it'. See also qaḫbata, below.

It is either to be identified with qaḫbata (see above), or with Amh. qaḫbīlātā 'be swollen' (from ḫf). For the vocalic initial becoming q, we can compare 'representation' in (ta)qarmaḫ (see below). This would represent an inverse process since normally q can become ḫ or zero in several Ethiopic languages. For qaḫbīlātā 'be swollen' is a misprint for Amh. qaḫbīlātā 'be swollen'.

meaning: 'be afflicted with dropsy, (T) be distended (stomach), be inflated', qaḫdafa 'afflicted with dropsy', qaḫdafa 'blow up (stomach), blow up'
origin_explanation: Smorrc: Frankel 1898:75 connects it with Syr. qaḫdafa 'blow', 'collect, run together (liquids, secretions)'. Emorrc: is perhaps the root of qaḫdafa 'water, build'. 193: 207 connects it with Tna. qaḫdafa 'build water'; also Amh. qaḫdafa 'qā-ḫā-dā'.

meaning: 'prepare, make ready', qaḫdafa 'ready, be prepared, be ready, (T.M) 'be strong, prevail'
origin_explanation: to be connected with Or. qaḫdafa 'be ready'.

meaning: 'eat at the expense of others, be nourished from house to house, be supported (T), act, participle'
origin_explanation: Emorrc: Amh. qaḫdafa 'be supported', while meaning W.W. 1928 considers it a poetical gwe form. R. 778 states that it is an Amh. expression. The form is strange. It is worth noting that the meaning of the Geez verb is expressed in Amharic by qaḫdafa.
from Ar. qaḫdafa; also in Har. qaḫdafa. Amh. qaḫdafa. See also qaḫdafa.
Dillmann 1395 expresses reservations concerning this noun. See also qaḫdafa, qaḫdafa.

meaning: 'cut, cut open, tear apart, make a hole, puncture'
origin_explanation: Smorrc: Ar. qaḫdafa 'cut'. Heb. qaḫdafa 'cut open'. Gaf. qaḫdafa. Syr. qaḫdafa 'cut'. Akk. qaḫdafa 'cut'. G. qaḫdafa. Sar. qaḫdafa. Gur. qaḫdafa. Akk. qaḫdafa 'chop'. Emorrc: Or. qaḫdafa and G. qaḫdafa (augmented by r). Tna. qaḫdafa 'cut, tear'. Te. qaḫdafa. Amh. qaḫdafa. Arg. qaḫdafa. Gur. qaḫdafa. Gaf. qaḫdafa. Har. qaḫdafa. Amh. qaḫdafa 'cut'. Gur. qaḫdafa 'hole', 'passed into Cushitic'. Bil. qaḫdafa. Kham qaḫdafa. See also qaḫdafa, qaḫdafa, below.
qaḫdafa 'row'.

meaning: 'water, pour out, empty by withdrawing the contents, pour into, copy (a section of a book)', qaḫdafa (K) 'causative', qaḫdafa 'passive of qaḫdafa', qaḫdafa 'draw water severally', (Gr.179) be drained, be drained', qaḫdafa (L1) 'drawing water', qaḫdafa 'drawn, poured, infused', qaḫdafa (L1) 'drawing water (N)', 'a sip, a gulp, gulp, goblet', qaḫdafa 'which is drawn, excerpt, extract', qaḫdafa 'well bucket', qaḫdafa 'act of drawing, act of pouring', maqaḫdafa, maqaḫdafa (T.M) 'means of drawing water, vase, water jar', maqaḫdafa 'place from which water is drawn, well, marketplace (that is, place where profits are drawn)', maqaḫdafa (Gr.179) 'urn', qaḫdafa 'a qaḫdafa 'jar', 'jug, bucket'
origin_explanation: Smorrc: Ar. qaḫdafa 'lade out', qaḫdafa 'drinking bowl'. Sar. qaḫdafa 'cup, vessel'. Emorrc: Te qaḫdafa 'draw water'. Tna. qaḫdafa. Har. qaḫdafa. Amh. qaḫdafa. Amh. qaḫdafa. Gaf. qaḫdafa. Gur. qaḫdafa. Passed into Cushitic: Be. qaḫdafa. Bil. qaḫdafa. G. qaḫdafa.

meaning: 'pierce, make a hole', maqaḫdafa (D L1) 'instrument for boring, drill, instrument for torturing', 'for consecration, dedication', maqaḫdafa is a metallic-us in relation to qaḫdafa III.
origin_explanation: Smorrc: Ar. qaḫdafa 'bore, pierce'. Soq. qaḫdafa 'pierce'. Heb. qaḫdafa 'bore, drill'. Syr. qaḫdafa 'pierce'. Md. qaḫdafa (with metathesis). Emorrc: Te qaḫdafa 'detach flesh from a bone'. See also qaḫdafa I, and qaḫdafa III.

Emorrc: Amh. qaḫdafa 'cause diarrhea', possibly from the original meaning of piercing the stomach (see qaḫdafa II). There is also the possibility of connecting it with G. qaḫdafa 'burn' (see below); cp. qaḫdafa 'fever'.

Smorrc: Gur. qaḫdafa 'flint'. Ar. (Syria) qaḫdafa 'kindle, flare up' (augmented by r), qaḫdafa 'gammish'. Heb. qaḫdafa 'be kindled, glow, burn'. Amh. qaḫdafa 'pistol'. Aram. qaḫdafa 'flare up, be inflamed'.

meaning: 'go before, come before, precede, begin, have priority, be first, be ahead of, surpass', 'with another verb it means "do first" what is expressed by the verb e.g. qaḫdama 'sow first' is 'preceded', qaḫdama (M) 'make to be first', qaḫdama 'give precedence, cause to precede, place first, place ahead, set before, happen first', 'with another verb or a nominal form it means "do first, do previously" what is expressed by the verb (e.g. qaḫdama nagīra 'he foretold, he said previously')', taqaḫdama 'precede, occur first, precede, hasten', taqaḫdama 'precede one another, go out to meet', taqaḫdama (Gr.179) 'choose ahead of time', qaḫdama 'first, previous, ancient, original, former, earliest, antecedent, predecessor, beginning, firsting (e.g. qaḫdama 'first fruit'), 'in the first place, at first, in the beginning, firstly, formerly, before, once, previously; for qaḫdama, see also below', (ta-qaḫdama 'of old, previously'), (am-qaḫdama 'of old, previously'), maqaḫdama 'qaḫdama wa-za-qaḫdama 'the Old and the New Testaments', qaḫdama 'beginning, precedence, priority', qaḫdama 'Saturday', qaḫdama 'that precedes', qaḫdama 'first Saturday (Sabbath)', (L1) qaḫdama 'Sabbath')

'being before (N), beginning, formerly', qaḫdama 'in the first place, at the first time, in former days, earlier, before, formerly, sooner, once', qaḫdama 'first, at the beginning, earlier, previously, formerly', qaḫdama 'former, ancient, previous, preceding', qaḫdama 'beginning, front, façade, start, precedence', qaḫdama (prep. adv.) 'before, in front, in the presence of, formerly, aforetime, at first, first of all, previously, beforehand, in advance, eastward', 'the suffix pronouns are attached to the base qaḫdama', (wa-qaḫdama 'before, from the presence of, formerly, before (prep.), prior to, sooner than'), (maqaḫdama-zi), (maqaḫdama-zini 'previously, formerly'), (ḫa-qaḫdama, see qaḫdama), qaḫdama 'forefathers, ancients, ancestors, men of old, nobles', (daw qaḫdama 'patriarchs'); qaḫdama 'first fruits', qaḫdama 'precedence, priority, primary, beginning, primordial state, presence', qaḫdama 'ancient, first, former, primary, eastern', qaḫdama 'ancient, first, former', qaḫdama 'precedence, priority, beginning, primordial existence', qaḫdama (L1) 'priority, precedence', maqaḫdama, maqaḫdama 'beginning, introduction, preface, foreword', maqaḫdama 'choicest, chief', (maqaḫdama 'first, (adv), beforehand, in the first place, before, above all'), (maqaḫdama (K) 'who precedes'), maqaḫdama (L1) 'first of all'
origin_explanation: Smorrc: Ar. qaḫdama. Sar. qaḫdama. Heb. qaḫdama 'be in front'. Ph. qaḫdama 'precede'. Aram. qaḫdama 'precede'. Ug. Ph. qaḫdama 'in front of'. Md. qaḫdama 'before'. Akk. qaḫdama 'former (times)'. Emorrc: Tna. qaḫdama 'precede'. Te. Arg. qaḫdama. Gur. Amh. qaḫdama. Har. qaḫdama.
ETHIOPIC: Tna. qäfäda 'tie, bind', Amh. qäffädä, qäfäddädä. See also qafaza.
perhaps from Gk. *skyphidion 'small goblet' (from skyphos).
see qafädē.

meaning: 'be hard, be tough, be thick, be cruel'
origin_explanation: SEMETIC: Ar. qafqa 'contract, shrink' (hence 'become hard'), Syr. qap (qapp) 'condense' (Dillmann 478). ETHIOPIC: perhaps Amh. qäfäf 'paunch, potbelly'.

meaning: 'a plot of land near a church, given by a founder for the maintenance of the church and the attendants'
origin_explanation: from Amh. qäffäf.

phonetics: qufähi (T), qufähi (L), qufähi (M., misprint for qufähi)
meaning: 'place in a church where the däbtära officiate'
origin_explanation: ETHIOPIC: Amh. qufähi (from Geez). Guidi 1906: 921 states that several sections of the church are "interpretazione singolari e forse in parte mistiche" in Aläqa Tayyä's Säwasəw; they end in -hi (see tadähi, tufähi, porähi, salämhi).

meaning: 'that which is overlaid, plate, beaten work'

origin_explanation: ETHIOPIC: Te. qafäla 'lock'; is to be identified with q"lf.
from Ar. qufl.
see maqfart.
ETHIOPIC: perhaps to be identified with G. rafaqa 'recline, lean back'.

meaning: 'dig a hole, dig out, hollow out, till fallow land, mark with notches, (figuratively) tame, inquire, examine, seek a prophecy'
origin_explanation: ETHIOPIC: Tna. q'äfära 'dig a hole, dig out', G. q'äfära, Arg. q'äfära, Gaf. q'äffära, Gur. qoffära. Possibly to be connected with Semitic hfr, hfr 'dig'; for q'h, cp. hadafa.
from Ar. qafriyy 'hermit'.
ETHIOPIC: Amh. qäffärä 'prick up the ears'.

meaning: 'unveil, uncover, open, unlock, denude, lay bare, expose'

origin_explanation: see laft.

meaning: 'silk shirt (given as a mark of distinction for meritorious service)'
origin_explanation: from Ar. quftän (of Persian, Turkish origin) through Amh. qäffän. For Ar. quftän, see Dozy 1845:162ff.
see kefäs, kefäz.
ETHIOPIC: Amh. qaqa, Tna. qaqa 'thorny bush', G. qog 'kind of tree'.
ETHIOPIC: Tna. qäham (Reinisch 1890:212), Te. 'aqham 'ant(s)', qomhay 'fourmi qui mange le miel'.
ETHIOPIC: Amh. qammo, qammo 'a tree whose fruit grows in bunches and is edible when cooked' (also Strelcyn 1973:no.180).

meaning: 'murmur, whisper, make a sign instead of speaking, clap the hands softly to draw someone's attention, knock lightly to draw attention, (K.T.Y) crack the knuckles, knock, tap idly and slowly as a sign of thought or sorrow, be pensive, (Y) raise the voice'
origin_explanation: SEMETIC: Ar. qahqaha 'roar with laughter', Syr. qahqah 'laugh, make a sound', qühqüha 'murmur'; related to PBH. kihkah 'clear the throat'. ETHIOPIC: Amh. q'aqq'a 'crack, knock', qaqqa 'crack'.
ETHIOPIC: Te. qahar 'acidity of the stomach, heartburn', Tna. qähar, qähar, Amh.Gur. qar, Tna. qäharä- 'have heartburn', Te. qäharä-. Amh. qarä-. See also qrh III.

meaning: 'be jealous, compete, contend, fight, strive to excel, boast'
'envy, be jealous, be zealous, be eager, compete, aspire, emulate, rival, contest, quarrel with one another, quibble, dispute, be contentious, boast, brag, strive to outvie, contradict'
'emulation, competition, contest, contention, strife, rivalry, envy, jealousy, desire for fighting, harshness, violence, boasting'

origin_explanation: ETHIOPIC: Te. (ta)qäwäha 'be avaricious, desire' (with metathesis).

meaning: A valley, lowland, low place, (T) river'
origin_explanation: ETHIOPIC: Tna. Amh. Arg. qʷalla 'lowland', Te. qʷalla, qʷallaqʷəl; from Cushitic: Kam. qʷallawa, Or. qolla. Note that Praetorius, ZDMG 47 (1893).391-2 connects qʷalla with qʷalay 'depth' (see qʷalaya I, below), and considers Cushitic to be of Semitic-Ethiopic origin. The connection with qʷalla, qolla 'roast' suggested by Guidi 1901:234 is unlikely. A connection with qʷaləgʷala II is to be considered.

meaning: 'voice, word, saying, speech, statement, discourse, command, order, sound, noise, expression, maxim, thing'; (qāla hade) 'see hade'; (qāla nagāśi [Lt] 'representative of a king'); (qāla tākā [Lt] 'envoy of a court'); ('assārtu qālāt 'the Ten Commandments'); qālāwi (Gr.454) 'one who has a voice'.
origin_explanation: SEMITIC: Ar. qāla 'say', Sar. qwl 'speaker', Heb. qōl 'voice', Syr. qāla, Ug.Ph. ql, Md. qala. Rabin HS:92 compares Akk. qālu which he renders 'shout'; however, it means 'pay attention to' (von Soden 895). ETHIOPIC: Te.Tna.Amh. qāl 'word', Gur. qāl, qe 'voice, thing'.
SEMITIC: Ar. qulla 'mass of people'. ETHIOPIC: Tna. qʷəlalā 'heap up, pile up', Amh. qʷəllālā.

Dillmann, ib. thinks that it transcribes Heb. qālī. However, the pronunciation of Geez qoli (with o) is different from that of Heb. qālī (unless it was pronounced qōlī). The G. qoli may be derived from G. qəlawa 'roast', below.

meaning: 'uncover, bare, open, remove, strip, unveil, (Gr.170) be torn, tear (intr), (Lt) lift (curtain), undo, pull aside'; taqəlā, passive. qəlāt, act. part. of qəl'a; qəlu 'uncovered, unveiled, removed, stripped'; *qəl, pl. qələlā (Lt) 'curtains'.
origin_explanation: SEMITIC: Ar. qala'a 'pluck out, extract, take off (clothes)', Heb. qāla 'uproot' (in Jer.10:18), accord-ing to Driver, JQR 28 (1937-8).107.

SEMITIC: Ar. qala'a 'pluck out', miqlā'a 'sling', Heb. qela', Syr. qel'a, Aram. qil'a, Ug. ql'. ETHIOPIC: The meaning of throwing from a sling in order to hit is related to that of Tna. qāl'e 'hit a ball with a stick', Amh. qālla.
ETHIOPIC: Te. qāla 'hard, rough, unfriendly'.

From Ar. qala'a 'fortress'; also Md.Syr. Cushitic: Som. qal'a, Be. qəl'a. For the (kalāta 'fortress') origin of Ar. qala'a, see Fraenkel 237, and W. Belardi, Istituto Orientale di Napoli.Anali. Sezione linguistica I (1959).147-150.

meaning: 'youth, boy, friend, servant, domestic'; qʷəl'aket 'maidservant'; qʷəl'awannā 'boyhood, youth, servitude'.
origin_explanation: SEMITIC: Akk. qallatu 'female slave' is connected with G. qʷəl'e (so also Holma, ZA 28[1914].161) rather than to be derived from qallu 'light' (root qll) as suggested by von Soden 894, and Leslau, JAOS 64 (1944).57. Ludolf 644 compares G. qʷəl'e with Ar. qallā 'lictor, attendant'. Note that Fraenkel 280 states concerning Ar. qallā 'die den arabischen Wortes erregt doch Verdacht gegen die Echtheit'. ETHIOPIC: Tna. qʷəl'a 'young boy'; denom. qʷəl'amā 'become a boy'. For the relation between the meanings 'young boy' and 'servant', cp. G. qulām, Amh. ləg. The root probably passed into Cushitic: Bil. qʷəl'a, Kham. qʷəl'ā. Reinisch 153 also compares Sa. qolā 'youth'.
see qəl'ā.

meaning: 'swerve, turn over, overturn, overthrow, turn in all directions, brandish a spear'; qʷəlabə 'overturning (n), (K*) deceit, fraud'.
origin_explanation: ETHIOPIC: Tna. qʷaləbe 'disclose, uncover', Te. qʷaləba, Amh. qʷallata 'cut off (ears from the stalk) > open, disclose'; passed into Cushitic: Bil. qʷaləba, qʷaləla. The root is related to kʷl (see kʷal'a).
From Amh. qʷallābā; also in Tna.Gur. qʷallābā, qʷallāba; passed into Cushitic: Qab. qallabbo 'feed', Or. qalabō 'rations'. See also qʷalapa I.
From Amh. qʷallābā; also in Tna. qʷallābā, Te. ləqʷāba (with metathesis), Har. qallāba, Gur. qallāba; passed into Cushitic: Qab. qallappa-yo 'catch a ball'. See also qʷalaba, and qʷalapa I.
From Ar. qʷalaba.
see qʷalaba III.
From Ar. qalb 'heart, mind'.

meaning: 'galbanum, resinous sap of an umbelliferous plant';
origin_explanation: From Gk. khalbanē 'resinous juice of all heal', transcription of Heb. helbənā (Masson 60); also in Syr. həlbanītā, Aram. hilbənētā (see Löw no. 115). See also kalbane.

meaning: 'collect water, draw water'; maqʷlad, maqʷlad, (M) maqʷlad (Lt) maqʷləd (pl. maqʷlād) 'bucket, bowl, basin, cup'; qʷalət 'water collector', Ar. (Dofar) miqlād, pl. of *maqʷlid 'basin'.
origin_explanation: SEMITIC: Ar. qalada 'collect water', Ar. (Dof.) məgālid, pl. of *məglid 'basin'.

meaning: 'pluck the eyebrows (because of an eye disease)';
origin_explanation: see qʷaldada.
From Amh. qʷallādā 'joke'.

meaning: 'beat with a stick, hit, pluck the eyebrows, cut the eyelid'; qʷaldad 'arch, circle'; qʷaldadā 'ayn, (T) qaldad 'eyebrow, eyelid', lit. 'the circle of the eye';
origin_explanation: the verb 'pluck the eyebrow' is probably a denomi-native, the meaning then extended to 'hit'. See qʷaldada II.
see qʷalafa II.

meaning: 'peel, decorticate'; qʷəllāf, qʷəl'āfī 'uncircumcised' (lit. 'who has the foreskin'); qʷəl'əfāt 'foreskin, prepuce, uncircumcised state';
origin_explanation: SEMITIC: Ar. qalaqa 'decorticate', qulfa (and qulfa) 'foreskin', Soq. qalfōh 'bark', Heb. qālap 'decorti-cate, skin', Aram. qəlap 'peel, strip', Syr. qəlap, qəlapta 'foreskin', Md. qlapta 'shell, hard skin', Akk. qālapu 'peel off', qilpu 'skin'. See also Noel-deke 1910:103, and Cohen 1926-27:104, n. 1. ETHIOPIC: Gur. qalf 'bark of a tree'. The root qlf. qʷəl'f is related to qrf (see qarafa, below). Arabic qalaqa passed into Som. qalaq 'rind, peel'.

meaning: 'close, lock'; 'aqʷəlafa (K*), causative; taqʷəlafa (T), pass. of qʷalafa; qʷəl'əf 'lock, bolt'; maqʷəlaf (Gr.171) 'bolt';
origin_explanation: SEMITIC: Ar. qafala (with metathesis) 'lock, latch', qaff 'lock' (n), considered by Fraenkel 16 an Aramaic loanword, contrary to Schulthess 62; Aram.Syr. qūplā 'lock, bolt', Hars. qefol 'lock'. ETHIOPIC: Tna. Amh. qʷəllāfā 'lock, button', Arg. qʷəllāfa, Te. qʷəllāfa, Har. (a)qʷəllāfa. Note that Te. and Har. have qfl as against qlf of the other Ethiopian languages. The root passed into Cushitic: Kam. qollaffo, Aw. kʷolefu, Sa. qʷalaf. On the root qʷəl'f, see Fleisch 360. See also qʷalapa, below, and qʷalafa, above.
metathesis of qʷalafa, above.
From Gk. kolophōniā 'resin'; also Ar. qalfūn, qalfū-niya (Dozy), Syr. qalfūnyā.

meaning: 'bud, calyx of flower, unripe fruit, flower that has not opened yet, cotton boll that is ready to burst open'; the passage of Ecclesiastes 12:5 where qʷəlḥ occurs is rendered 'caper, caperbud, caper berry'; OT renders it by by Amh. gʷəraʷa 'bush whose leaves have a purgative property' (see Strelcyn 1973:no. 117);
origin_explanation: ETHIOPIC: to be identified with Amh. qälləḥ 'shell, empty cartridge', so named because its shape resembles the calyx of a flower. Reinisch 1902:258 compares Som. qalaḥ 'hölzerner Trinkbecher'.

a reconstructed form of Amh. qʷəla (old Amh. qʷəlḥa). For the Cushitic origin of this root, see Cerulli 1951:488, under qʷəro. Note that the same consonants also occur in Akk. qʷəgallu 'sexual organ', considered a Sumerian loanword. Marcel Cohen would consider this noun 'un mot voyageur'.

meaning: 'be light, be easy, be slight, be swift, be rapid'; 'əqləla 'make light, make swift, accelerate, make agile, lighten, make easy, do easily, alleviate, mitigate, belittle, disdain, scorn, make light of, vilify'; 'əqəlləla 'consider light, lighten, (K*) despise, disdain'; possibly from Amharic; 'əqəläləla (Gr.169) 'have the habit of making light'; təqəl[l]ala 'be made light, be alleviated, be relieved'; 'əstəqəläləla 'lighten, alleviate, make easy, consider light, belittle, contemn, scorn, despise, revile, treat lightly'; qəlīl (fem. qəllīl) 'light, easy, swift, rapid, small, minor, of small value'; (bə-qəlīl 'easily'); qəläləle 'lightness, light-heartedness'; qəllat 'lightness, (K* Gr.169) swiftness, rapidity'; 'əqəlalo, in bə-əqəlalo (Lī) 'briefly, summarily'; məqlalī 'one who facilitates, one who lightens, one who alleviates';
origin_explanation: SEMITIC: Sar. qəlla (qəl) 'be light, be small', Sar. qəll 'little, few', Soq. qel(l) 'be small', Heb. qal 'light, easy', Aram. qəl, Md. qālīl 'light', Akk. qəlālu 'be light'. Geez 'əqləla 'vilify' < 'be little' is not to be connected with Heb. qillel 'curse', Ug. ql-t 'shame', as suggested by Gordon 478. ETHIOPIC: Tna.Gur. qälälä 'be light', Te. qälläla, Har. qäläla, Amh. qällälä. Arg. qälləla, Gaf. qällīya 'light', passed into Cushitic: Bil. qäläl 'be light', Kham. qal, Sa. qalal. See also qəlləla I.
a root augmented with -ya from 'əqləla 'make light, make easy' (see qəlləla, above). For a similar formation, see qəzzəy, below.
it has the form of a reduplicative stem of the Amharic type from qəlləla (see below). Stade 34 states that this form expresses an intensive analyzing the form.
the form gives the impression of rendering the Amharic form 'əqləlla (with an augmented -ya) 'turn reddish', a reduplicative stem of Amh. qälləla. For Amh. qälləla, see qyḥ, qeḥa.

meaning: 'write, color, stain' (denominative of qəlam); təqələma (K*), passive; qəlāmī (K*), act. part. of qələma; qəlum (K*) 'written, stained'; qəlam (pl. 'əqlām) 'ink, color, inkstand, pen, reed, stalk, letter, words, (K*) knowledge derived from books'; (zəqəlam [Lī] 'colored'); 'əqlām (pl. of qəlam) 'writing materials'; qəlmat 'stain, blemish';
origin_explanation: SEMITIC: Ar. qalam 'reed' (from Gk. kalamos, kalu-mə, see Lokotch, no. 1023), Sar. qəlm 'calamus aro-md, Md. qəlmiā. The Greek form is reflected in Aram. qulmōs, and in Syr. qālāmūs. According to Fraenkel 246, Ar. qalam 'reed' is borrowed from Aram. qulmōs, while Noeldeke 1910:50 derives Ar. qalam from G. qalam. It is possible that the mean-ings of Geez 'ink, color' are to be separated from the meanings 'reed, stalk'. In this case, qəlam 'reed, stalk' would come directly either from Greek through Ar. qalam, whereas qəlam 'ink, color' is Ethiopian root. Since, however, 'reed' and 'ink' are used in writing, it is also possible that they go back to a single root qəlam. ETHIOPIC: Tna. qäläm 'color, dye, ink', Te. qälläma 'paint, color' (v), qäläm 'ink, color', Amh. qällämä 'paint, color, dye, write in ink', qäläm 'dye, ink, stain, color', Gur. qäläm 'color', Har. qäläm 'reed' passed into Cushitic: Som. qalin 'writing reed', Sa.Af. qalam, Be. qalam. See also qəlanpe.

meaning: 'catch an animal with a rope, twist the neck';
origin_explanation: from Amh. qʷälämmämä 'twist the neck (of animal and make it fall)';
ubu qələmsīs, John, considered the author of the Apocalypse'; corruption of Gk. apokalypsis.
see also qələmtədä.

ETHIOPIC: Amh. qälämsīs 'spark, glowing of a hot griddle'; probably from G. in view of the preserved Wajnberg 1937b:2 connects G. qələmsəs with Tna. qʷəlləʿe alä 'scintillate'; the Geez root would then be augmented by m.
from Gk. klēmatida, acc. sg. from klēmatis 'branch, vine branch'. See also qələmsədä.

meaning: 'pond, water hole (where sheep are washed before being sacrificed)';
origin_explanation: from Gk. kolymbēthra 'swimming pool' through Chr. Ar. qulmbatra.
from Lat. calendae through Gk. kalandai, acc. of kalandai, same meaning; also Aram. qalandās (Krauss 546). See also qəlanton.
is perhaps a doublet of qəlam (see qəlam, above) with alternation of labials; see also qəlapəla II.

from Ar. qalansuwa 'cap worn under the turban' (for which see Dozy 1845:365ff); possibly corrupt from Gk. khlamys 'upper garment of wool'. Amharic transcribes Ar. qalansuwa as qälänswa, qälänswa, qälänswät (Isenberg 69). See also qəlasīn, below.
from Gk. kalandōn (gen. of kalandai 'first day of the month'); see also qələndəs, above.

meaning: 'devour, swallow, bite, gnaw, chew, sting, catch with the mouth something that has been thrown, catch with an open mouth, (T) snatch away, lacerate'; it covers the meanings of qəlabəla II 'nourish', and qəlabəla III 'catch in midair';
origin_explanation: also in Cushitic: Sa. qəlab 'devour'. See also qəlpəpa I, and qəlpəwa.
is a variant of qəlama. See also qəlanpe.
SEMITIC: Grimme 1914:261 connects it with Ar. qabala 'tie the shoe'. The root is related to qʷəlafa II 'lock, close'.

meaning: same meanings as qəlapəla I, with reduplication of the last radical;
origin_explanation: is related to qərpəpa (below), with alternation l:r.
would be a misprint for məlpəppo?

meaning: 'mix up, confuse, blend together, destroy, abolish';
origin_explanation: ETHIOPIC: Amh. qäläqälä 'mix, stir', Arg. qäläqälä, Har. (tä)qälqälä 'be mixed together, be intermingled', liqäläqä (with metathesis) 'mix solids'. The root is related to the meanings of qlql II.

meaning: 'move, shake, swing, quake, agitate, make tremble, vacillate, totter, stagger'; some of the verbs are both transitive and intransitive; (za˓iyyanqalqäl 'immovable goods'); tanqalqala (Lt) 'totter'; ˓anqalqäli (M), act. part. of ˓anqalqala; ˓anqalqalo (Lt) 'movement'; naqalqäl 'movement, motion, shaking, agitation, commotion'; manqalqäl 'one who moves, shakes, stirs';
origin_explanation: SEMITIC: Ar. qalqala 'agitate, move', also qaliqa 'be agitated, be troubled', Heb. qilqel 'shake', Syr. qalqel 'throw into confusion', Aram. qälaq 'throw'. ETHIOPIC: Tna. ˓anqalqälä 'tremble, be agitated, shake, vibrate', Te. ˓anqalqälä 'shake', Amh. tän-qäläqälä 'be restless, roam about' (probably also q'ällä 'wag the tail', Praetorius 1879:132), Gur. (a)qäläqälä, anqäläqälä 'move, shake, swing'. The root passed into Cushitic: Bil. anqalqal, Som. qalqal. The root is related to qlql I, and q˓alq˓ala I. See also Conti 1980:87.

meaning: 'brandish, agitate, shake, vibrate (spear, stick; tr. intr), throw (a spear)'; ˓aq˓alq˓ala, same meanings;
origin_explanation: to be identified with qlql II, ˓anqalqala 'move, shake, swing'.

meaning: 'bring down, send down, (T) go downhill, hang upside down, turn upside down, push down'; taq˓alq˓ala 'lean, lean downward, go down (into a deep place), decline (sun), be lowered'; q˓alqul 'sloping, rolling down, inclining downward'; q˓alq˓ala (Lt) 'upside down'; q˓alq˓al (Lt) 'slope'; q˓alq˓alat (K*) 'going down (n), lowering, declining'; q˓alq˓olit 'steepness, slope, declivity'; q˓alq˓alita 'downward, upside down, head downward';
origin_explanation: ETHIOPIC: Tna. q˓älq˓alä 'slope down, decline', Amh. täq˓älaqälä 'be put upside down, be downward slope, declivity', Amh. q˓älq˓alä. See also qälqal, q˓alla.

meaning: 'sloping place, precipice, cliff, ravine, (T.Gr.170) edge of a cliff';
origin_explanation: ETHIOPIC: Tna. qälqäl 'edge of a cliff'. See also q˓alq˓ala II.

ETHIOPIC: Tna. q˓älq˓äl 'äL, Te. qälqänqal, Amh.Arg. q˓alq˓al, Gur. qulq˓al; from Cushitic: Qem. K˓älk˓äla, Som. qulqäl; Bil. q˓älanq˓al seems to be borrowed from Tigre even though Tigre does not have a labiovelar.
from Gk. kylikhnion 'small cup' (diminutive from kylikhnē).
either directly from Gk. kolokasiā 'Egyptian bean', or through Ar. qulqās, qulqās; also in Aram. qolqas, Syr. qulqāsa, for which see Krauss 507-8, Löw 240-1.
from Gk. klērikos 'clergyman'.

meaning: 'bend down'; taqalqa (K*) 'be bent'; taqäläsa 'fight, wrestle, contest'; ˓astaqäläsa, caus. of taqäläsa; taqäläso 'contest';
origin_explanation: ETHIOPIC: Amh. qälläsa 'bend down'. As for G. taqäläsa, its original meaning is that of being bent, twisted, linked together, as in wrestling; cp. also Tna. täqäläsa 'turn one against the other, wrestle', qäläsa 'turn, go around'.
metathesis of qilänos, above.
from Amh. qälläṭä 'be melted'; also Gur. qäläṭä.
ETHIOPIC: Amh. qäläṭo 'kind of cake'.
SEMITIC: Ar. qaltafa 'agility'; the same consonants occur in Ar. falqata 'hurry'. ETHIOPIC: Tna.Amh.Arg. qälṭaffa 'fast, rapid, efficient'; passed into Cushitic: Kham. qilṭef 'busy, active'.

meaning: 'roast, parch'; taqalawa, taq˓alawa, (M.K) taqalwa 'be roasted, be parched, be burnt, be branded'; qaləw 'roasted, parched'; qoli 'parched grain' (see above); qälyä (M) 'parched grain'; qälyähu (T) 'parched grain';
origin_explanation: SEMITIC: Ar. qala (qäly) 'fry, roast', Soq. qale, qala (qäly), Aram.-Syr. qala, Md. qla 'burn, roast', Akk. qalû. ETHIOPIC: Tna. qäläwä 'roast', Te. qäla (qäly), Arg. q˓älla 'parch grain, roast', Gaf. qolla, Har. qala. Reinisch 1895:25 compares Be. enkaliw from emkaliw < me-kaliw. As for Bil. maqlo 'pan' it is borrowed from Ar. maqlā(t).
see qolawqa.
from Gk. klauthmonos (gen. sg. of klauthmōn) 'weeping'.

meaning: 'be deep, (L) search, search thoroughly, scrutinize, penetrate, understand, reveal'; ˓aqalaya (K*) 'deepen, make deep'; qäläy 'deep (like an abyss)'; qälay (pl. qäläyät) 'depth (of river, sea), ocean, gulf, abyss, pit, ravine, gully, underworld, lower regions, (Lt) pool, pond'; qälyat (Lt) 'depth';
origin_explanation: ETHIOPIC: Te. qälay 'pond', Tna. qälay 'lake, sea', Amh. qälay 'deep and wide body of water' (from Geez. Praetorius, ZDMG 47 (1893).391-2 derives qälay from Cushitic: Or. qilē, qälä 'cave', Sa. qale 'depth'. See also qäly, ˓aqälayä, and q˓ällä.

meaning: 'bite, stimulate, offend, convict, condemn, reprove, make critical remarks, insult, be distrustful';
origin_explanation: Dillmann 415 derives these meanings from an original meaning 'roast, burn', the root qly being related to qlw (see qalawa).

meaning: 'amputate, hew off, cut off, prune'; q˓alz (K*) 'pruning', (Gt) name of a season; maq˓alaz, pl. maq˓aläz (D.T), maqlaz (T.K.M) 'axe';
origin_explanation: SEMITIC: Ar. qalaza 'hit', Ar. (Syria) qalaz 'remove'. ETHIOPIC: Amh. q˓älläzä 'prune, cut off thorns'. See also maqlaza.
from Cushitic: Be. kama 'necklace'. Dillmann 417 suggests, with reservations, Syr. qämā 'bit, muzzle', from Gk. kēmos. See also gämä.

meaning: 'eat grain or other fodder, graze'; ˓aqamha, causative; also 'feed, give fodder'; maqmäḥ 'pasture, stable, stall, manger, crib, (T) grazing place';
origin_explanation: SEMITIC: Ar. qamḥ 'wheat', qamḥa 'eat parched barley or wheat', Ar. (Dat) qamah 'eat'. Dillmann 417 (followed by Buhl 716, and J. Aro, ZDMG 115[1963]:476) compares Heb. qemaḥ 'flour'. ETHIOPIC: the root is represented as qmḥ and qmh; thus Tna. q˓ämaḥä 'swallow, devour', Te. qäḥama, Arg. qäḥama, Amh. qäma (from qhm), but Har. qämaḥa 'take a mouthful'. The root is common with Cushitic: Sa. qam 'eat', Chew. qäb. Cäti qämma 'chew the čät-plant'. Or. qäma 'crunch something dry'. See also qäḥama, above, and Sasse 25.

meaning: 'produce, yield, fruit, leguminous plant'; 'waqmäh (pl. of qamḥ) 'fruit trees, all kinds of cereals or fruit'; '(aṣadä 'aqmäh 'garden'.

origin_explanation: (K.*Mk) ETHIOPIC: Amh. qämməḥo 'serious but not contagious disease of the throat in which the throat swells considerably and pus forms in the area of infection'. A reduplication of this root occurs in Qem. qamqäw.

(L) 'delouse' (denominative). SEMITIC: Ar. qaml-a 'a louse', SAr. qlm-t 'insect pest', Heb. qəmal 'be infested with lice', Aram. qalmətā, qalmətā 'louse', Syr. qalmā, Akk. kalmatu related to Heb. kinnām 'gnat', Soq. konem 'louse', Mh. konem. ETHIOPIC: Tna. q°mäl 'louse', Te. qäməl-ät, Amh. Arg.Gur. qəmal, Har. qumäy; perhaps also Cushitic *kilm 'tick', for which see Sasse 13.
(Lt) Probably from Ar. qumqum 'long-necked bottle'; from Gk. koukkoumion 'jar' (from Latin cucuma); also PbH. qümqüm (see Fraenkel 70, Krauss 511).

meaning: 'build an arch, arch over, vault over, frame a house, round off, (T.Y.M) stretch, stretch out (a hand or a spear in order not to let someone through)'; 'aqammara, see qamara; taqamra, taqamara, passive; also (Lt) 'rise to a peak, reach the zenith'; qamäri (Gr.), act. part. of qamara; qomur 'arched, vaulted, round plate around the central pillar of a house'; qamar (pl. 'aqmār) 'vault, arch, firmament, vaulted structure, chamber, juncture, (K*) join-ing of beams, (Gr.418) assemblage of beams'; the basic meaning is that of 'round > arch, firma-ment, circle, cycle'. There is the possibility of con-necting qamar 'circle, cycle' with qmr II, qamar 'calculation, computation'. Note that PbH. qāmar 'build an arch, a roof' is considered a Greek (kama-ra 'vaulted chamber') loanword. ETHIOPIC: Tna. qämärä 'build an arch'. Praetorius 1879:100 compares Amh. qämär 'yoke', particu-larly in view of the meaning 'juncture'.

'count, com-pute, number, divide, separate, (T) prepare'; taqammara (K*), passive; qammari (Gr.172), act. part. of qammara; qammur (K*) 'computed, counted'; qamar (pl. 'aqmār) 'count, number, calcula-tion, computation, division, section, cycle'; 'aqmār (pl) 'computation tables, canons'.
origin_explanation: (T.Y.M) ETHIOPIC: Tna. qämärä 'compute, reckon by the calendar', Amh. qämmärä. The verb is perhaps a denominative from qamar 'moon' (see below) which serves in the computation. For qamar, see P. Mauro da Leonessa, 1943:309ff.
(Lt)
(C) qamaräwi 'lunar'. From Ar. qamar 'moon'. See also qmr II.
(K*C) maqämär, (Lt) 'əl-maqämär 'gambling game played with pebbles'. From Ar. qimār, qumār (Lane quoted in Dozy II 404); also Te. qämaren 'gamblers', Amh. qumär 'gambling'.
(Lt) From Amh. qämmäsä 'taste'; also Tna. qämäsä. See also qsm I.
(Y.M) 'put on a shirt' (denominative). variant of qamiṣ (see below).

meaning: 'prior, church administrator, head priest, father con-fessor of the bishop, archpriest'; qommasa (K*) 'become an archpriest' (denomi-native); taqommasa (K*), same; qumsənnā (K*) 'office of the qomos'.
origin_explanation: (Gr.173) qamäs From Chr.-Ar. qummuş < Gk. hēgoumenos (Graf also in Syr.PbH. qūmīs, Tna.Amh. qomos. See qomos. For more details on the func-tion of the qomos, see L. Ricci, RSE 22 (1966).95-98. See also mq°s.
(D 1402) either directly from Ar. qumāš (of Turkish Vollers 1897:309), or through Amh. qumāš; Neo-Syr. qumāš, Tna.Te. qəmāš.

meaning: 'shirt, sleeved reaching to the feet' (it is the principal under-vestment of priests)
origin_explanation: (K*) see Hammerschmidt 1970:152; From Ar. qamīṣ (from Gk. kamision 'shirt', Fraenkel 44-5); also in Syr. qumīṣtā, Ug. According to Jeffery 243 the Greek noun passed into Syriac and Ethiopic, and G. qamīṣ is in all probability the source of the Arabic noun. Littmann 1924:22 states: "Die alten Araber haben das von den römischen Soldaten in Syrien über-men". ETHIOPIC: Tna.Amh. qamīṣ 'dress', Te. qämīṣ, Gur. qamīṣ; passed into Cushitic: Bil.Sa.Af. qamīṣ, Or. qamīṣī. See also qamiṣ.
(L) see qamṭarä, below.

(K*) taqammata 'sit down, remain'; taqmät (K*) 'diarrhea'. From Amh. täqämmätä 'sit', täqmaṭ 'diarrhea', Tna. täqämmätä, Har. aqmäṭa 'rest on a base' in Cushitic: Sa. kames 'sit down', Be. kemis. SEMITIC: related to Akk. kamāšu 'bend the knee down, squat', PbH. qamsūs 'bent up' (E.Y. Kutscher, Leshonenu 31[1967].115-6), Ug. qmṣ (quot 509).

meaning: 'till the ground thoroughly, harrow, put the ground in good order, level a field (T.Y) knead'
origin_explanation: (T) SEMITIC: Dillmann 419 compares the G. root with Ar. qaṭa'a 'cut' with an original n becoming m; n becoming m, see Dillmann 1907:65. ETHIOPIC: Te. qämṭəṣä 'bela 'till the ground thorough-ly'. See also (a)qamṭawa.
(Sw. in D 420) (T)

meaning: 'make furrows, (T.Y) give extra touches to something, (Y) ad-just, arrange, knead, rub, plow for the first time'; see also qmṭ I.

origin_explanation: (nəsr qänä; see nəsr) ETHIOPIC: Tna.Amh. qäna; see qnw II.
(T.M) perhaps connected with Amh. quni 'perfume' if the olive oil is meant to be used as a perfume.

meaning: 'be envious, be jealous, be eager, emulate, imitate'; 'aqnaqa 'make jealous, move to envy, incite to zeal, inspire, incite to emulation'; taqänqäwa 'be envious of one another, emulate'; 'astaqänqäwa 'provoke mutual jealou-sy, make one another envious, incite to envy'; qanäwi 'envious, jealous, zealot'; qan, qan, (K*) qani 'envy, jealousy, zeal, emulation, ardor'; qanwat, qanwat, (T) qan'at 'jealousy, envy, emula-tion, rivalry, ardor, indignation'; taqanqo 'mutual envy'
origin_explanation: (K*) SEMITIC: Brockelmann, ZS 8 (1932). 115 (following G. Hoffmann) connects it with Ar. qanara 'be blood-red, be deep-red', Soq. qn 'be jealous', Heb. qinne 'be envious', Aram.Ug. qn 'be jealous', Syr. qenne (')ṭā 'grudge', Md. qina 'envy, jealousy'. ETHIOPIC: Te. qänə'a 'be envious', Tna. qänəwe, Amh. Arg.Gur. qänna, Har. qaňə'a, Amh. qänawi 'zealot'; taken from Geez. The root passed into Cushitic: Bil. qan 'envy', Sa. qana. See also qananäwi.

meaning: 'oil of myrrh, galbanum'; 'put on new clothes'; perhaps Te. (ə)qändə'a (qndw, qndy) 'be content, be satisfied, be satisfied with one's actions or behavior > become haughty'; Te. (tä) qännägä 'be coquettish' is considered by Littmann-Höfner 253 an Arabic (qaniga) loanword. See also q°ndw.
origin_explanation: SEMITIC: Ar. qinna. Noeldeke 1910:43-4 states "man würde ohne weiteres annehmen, das jener (G. qənnə'at) zu den Arabern gekommen ist, wenn sich nur nachweisen hesse dass Afrika Galbanum erzeugen und ausführen könne".

meaning: 'put in good order'; 'straighten', Amh.Gur. qänna 'be straight', Har. qänna'a 'direct oneself, be well-mannered'. For more details, see Leslau 1979:485.
origin_explanation: ETHIOPIC: Tna. qänna 'be straight'. Te. qənnə'a 'straighten'. Amh.Gur. qänna 'be straight', Har. qänna'a 'direct oneself, be well-mannered'. For more details, see Leslau 1979:485.
from Gk. koinobion 'convent'.

meaning: 'lamp, sanctuary lamp, lantern, extreme unction'; (məşəfəqa qandil 'Ritual for the unction of the sick, prayers for extreme unction'); see Hammerschmidt, in Asfalg-Krüger 260;
origin_explanation: either directly from Gk. kandêle 'lamp' (from Lat. candela) or through Ar. qandil. According to Fraenkel 95, Gk. kandêle > Aram.Syr. qandilâ > Ar. qandil.
see qodräntis.

meaning: 'bread made of the seeds of the plant called muğa in Amharic, (T*) dagussa-millet'.
'strut, walk in a haughty or conceited way, (T) express anger through bodily motions (e.g. by taking short, quick steps)';
origin_explanation: see q°andaya.

meaning: see taq°anädaya; taq°anädaya 'be proud, be haughty, move with insolence, exult with insolence, strut, glorify oneself, boast, (Y) make derogatory remarks, deride';
origin_explanation: 'astaq°anädaya (K*), causative'; ETHIOPIC: Amh. (tä) qanäda 'swagger, walk while moving the entire body, flirt', Tna. (tä)q°anägäwä 'swagger'.

meaning: (pl. qannona) 'canon, dogma, constitution, rule, rite, penance imposed by a priest, devotion (religious), prayer, supplication' (see Walker 73);
origin_explanation: qannona 'establish a canon, impose penance, accomplish a satisfying action, do penance (denominative)'; taqannona 'receive penance'; from Gk. kanôn 'ecclesiastic law, decree', also Ar. qanûn; in Ethiopic: Te. qänona, Tna. qännona. The root passed into Cushitic: Som. qanûn.

probably from Ar. qunfud (with d > z) in Te.Tna. qanfaz. The root also occurs in Heb. qippôd, Syr. quppodâ, Md. qunpud, from qpd 'go back to qpd, qfd) 'contract, roll up'. The Ethiopic noun passed into Cushitic: Be. kunfed, Sa. qunfid. See on this root Wagner 102.
see qənämomu.
from Syr. qnûm > Ar. 'iqnûm, 'uqnûm, qnûm.

phonetics: qanänomu, qanänəmu, qanänəmo, qanänəmon, qanmos, qanänəmos
origin_explanation: from Gk. kinnámômon, nom. acc.sg. 'a superior kind of cassia'. For the Semitic origin of the Greek noun, see Littmann 1926:417, Masson 50. The root occurs in Heb. qinnâmôn, Aram.Syr. qinnâmâ, Amh. qänänəmos.
see qənämomu.
see qənämomu.
from Chr.-Ar. qunûmîyya 'place where church objects are kept' derived by Graf 93 from Gk. oikonomía 'management of household'; also in Amh. q°anämat, qullamat.
see qannona.

SEMITIC: probably Ar. qamas 'small insect', Sh. qies (from qms) 'small bug' (W. Müller). ETHIOPIC: Tna. q°ansi, q°anči 'flea', Te. qäs, qäz, Amh.Arg. qunčəča, Har. qunčä', Gur. qunčä'; derived from qamasa 'jump, leap' (see above). It could also be derived from the related root qnt. qənti 'pinch' (for the root in the various Ethiopian languages, see Leslau 1963:127). The root also occurs in Cushitic: Qab. qinči-ta 'mosquito'. Cerulli 1938a:78 connects it with Sid. korčo, Gan. kənäko, Kham. qəmsa.
ETHIOPIC: Amh. q°anätälä 'scatter, disperse'.

ETHIOPIC: Te. qansəl 'jackal', Amh. q°anṣəl 'fox', from Geez. Brockelmann 1950:25 considers it a Cushitic loanword: Bil. q°ansalä, Kham. q°asəlä. The connection with G. qanasa 'jump', Ar. qanasa 'hunt' (originally 'jump upon'), as suggested by Hommel 379 is doubtful.
probably Amh. qantot 'luxury', yəqantot əqa 'luxury items', e.g. 'perfume'; or could it be a misprint for qənwät? (W. Müller). See also qantawa, below.

meaning: 'put on a belt, gird on'; 'aqnata 'gird, put a girdle around'; taqanta, passive; also 'gird on'; qanati, act. part. of qanata; qənut 'girded'; qənät (pl. qənäwät) 'girdle, sash, belt (serves as vestment of the priest)'; see Hammerschmidt 1970:153; maqnat, maqannat, maqannati, maqannati (T.M.Li) 'belt';
origin_explanation: SEMITIC: possibly Ar. (ta)nattaqa 'gird oneself', with metathesis and emphatization of t to t by assimilation (Dillmann 443). The connection with Ar. qinna(t), pl. qinnan 'strand of a rope' suggested by Rabin 1978:400 is unlikely. ETHIOPIC: Te. qänätä 'gird on', Tna. (tä)qännätä, Amh. qännätä 'put on a belt', mäqännät 'women's belt'.
see qnn I.
see qənzäy, below.
ETHIOPIC: Tna.Amh. qənzä 'calf'.
ETHIOPIC: Tna. qənzä, qänzi 'violent pain', Amh. qənza.

meaning: 'lock of hair, ringlet, tuft of hair, braid, plaited hairdo'
origin_explanation: SEMITIC: Ar. qurza, qunzuwa, qunduwa 'lock of hair', Ar. (Dat) qurza'a. On Ar. qurza, see Landberg 1923:1331.

while G. qoppon is borrowed from Cushitic (Or. kopē), even though the final -n remains to be explained (so also Praetorius, ZDMG 43[1889].323), the root is common to Semitic and Cushitic: Ar. qabqab, qirqab (Dozy) 'wooden clog', Har. kubkab, Te. qərəqab (from qəb), Gur. k'əbe; Cushitic: Be. kobā, Som. kab, Had. kobe'e. As for the roots of Be. kerkāb, Sa. qirqāb, they are borrowed from Tigre.
perhaps to be connected with Amh. kuf čämma 'leggings'.
probably a misprint for qasäpe.

meaning: 'chew on food that is hard, grind'
origin_explanation: see qarpaра 'bite, chew', below.

phonetics: qepros, qepərson, qepəros, qepras (and others) qepros
origin_explanation: from Gk. kyparissos 'cypress', kyparissinos '(made) of cypress wood'.
from Gk. kopros, same.
None provided.

meaning: 'hard, be dry, be stiff, be benumbed, become weak, be emaciated, be haggard'
origin_explanation: ETHIOPIC: Te. qärqä'a 'become wrinkled', alä 'be crisp', qaqqa 'dry out (throat, from water)', (tän)qaqqa 'be dried out'. Reinisch 1902:32 compares Som. ingēg 'be drying up'. Or. gogä.

meaning: 'be perforated, be peeled, be cleft, be hairless, be covered with blisters'
origin_explanation: ETHIOPIC: Amh. qaqqa 'break open the egg (hen) to release the chick', q'aqq'a 'crack, take off the shell'.

for the idea of putting something into something, cp. G. (a')qabqaba 'drive a stake into the ground'. Amh. qäbäqäbä 'drive a nail into wood'. There is also the possibility of deriving qaqab from qabqab, a reduplicated root of qəb, borrowed from Cushitic: Sa. kabab 'sheath'.

SEMITIC, onomatopoetic: Syr. qūqānā 'partridge', Neo-Syr. qawqānā, Pbh. qūqīyyā. For the sound, see qoqha, below. ETHIOPIC: Tna.Te. qoqah 'partridge', Amh.Arg.Gur. qog, Gaf. quq'äqqa; also in Cushitic: Bil. qōqā, Be. k'am. Qoqaya. See also qoqha and qqw, below.
from qəhqəh; cp. Amh. qaqqa 'break the egg (hen) to release the chick', (tän)qaqqa 'break (intr., one's limbs)'.

SEMITIC: Ar. qāqa, qa'qa'a 'cackle', qa'qa'a 'make noise', Syr. qawqi. ETHIOPIC: Te. qa' bela 'crack aloud', qoq alä 'croak', Tna. qa' bäla, Amh. (an)qaqqa, qaqa Gur. q'a epä 'produce a sound', qawqaw bäla 'a noise and wake up others'. See also q'a 'raven', qoqäh 'francolin'.

SEMITIC: Ar. qalāqil 'small bells' (Dozy). ETHIOPIC: qel, with ē coming from qe, qi: Te. qalaq 'ring' (v), Amh. qaqəl 'bell', Tna. qaqəl, qasal. For q coming in Amharic, see Cohen 1939:3, Gurage, Leslau 1979:XXXIX. The root passed into Cushitic: Kham. qaqīl.
from Gk. kyknon, acc.sg. of kyknos 'swan'; also in Ar. qāqanass (Dozy), Syr. qūqnūs.
None provided.

SEMITIC, from qrqr (for the reduplication, see Noel-deke 1904:122), and cp. Ar. qarqara, a verb that expresses the noises of various animals, Heb. qirqer, Syr. qarqar. ETHIOPIC: Amh. q'äräqq'ärä 'rap lightly', Amh. qaqer 'kind of insect that makes noise during the rainy season'.

meaning: 'be avaricious, be stingy, be a miser, be sparing with, deprive, bereave'
origin_explanation: SEMITIC: Stade 19 connects it with Heb. qəy (nif'al) 'be collected', Pbh. qāwā (qwy) 'collect'. ETHIOPIC: Tna. qeqäyä, qägäyä 'be stingy', Amh. qiqy 'stingy' (from Gəəz), Amh. qäqqäta 'avarice, greed', from qqy.

from Ar. qār, qir 'bitumen', of Aram.-Syr. origin (Fraenkel 150); also in Md. qira, Akk. qiru 'asphalt', Amh. qar 'liquid pitch'. Zimmern 60 suggests, with reservations, an Akkadian origin of this root.
None provided.
None provided.

meaning: 'container serving as a measure, measure of wheat or flour'
origin_explanation: transcribes Heb. kor 'dry measure'; also Te. qor, Amh. qore 'plate' (undoubtedly serving as a measure). See qoros, below.

meaning: 'hit the head with a stick, (Lt) shear, clip'
origin_explanation: SEMITIC: Ar. qara'a 'hit', miqra'a 'whip', Heb. qara' 'tear', Md. qra. ETHIOPIC: Amh. q'ärra 'cut by puncturing a vein in the forehead'. The root is related to k'ar'a, above.
from Amh. q'ärr in rasä q'ärr. W. Müller suggests the possibility of its being derived from Gk. korys 'helmet'.

meaning: = Amh. əmb'ay 'a bush having a bitter red or yellow fruit'.
origin_explanation: None provided.

meaning: 'eyelid'; a secondary form of qərnəb, below.

'draw near, be near, approach. come close, be connected, be akin, be presented, be celebrated (Mass)'; qərabəa 'celebrate Mass, offer sacrifices'; qərrabəa 'cause to approach, bring near, bring closer, present, offer, administer the Holy Sacrament, give communion'; 'aqarrabəa 'bring near, offer sacrifices, administer the Eucharist'; taqarəbəa, taqarrabəa 'be offered, be presented'; taqarrabəa 'approach one another, come close together, adjoin, be neighboring, have sexual intercourse'; 'astaqarəbəa 'unite'; 'astaqarrabəa, caus. of taqarrabəa; qarabə (K*) act. part. of qarba; qərub 'near, adjacent, close, related, immediate, recent; proximity, vicinity'; (qəruba 'close to, close by, closely, at hand, recently, lately, nearly'); qərəbat 'proximity, closeness, union, vicinity, nearness, annexation, connection'; qərubəwi 'one who is close'; maqrab 'one who offers'; maqrab, maqrab 'near, neighboring, relative'; maqrabə 'one who brings near'; maqrab 'neighborhood, vicinity, objects connected with offering, vessel for incense, censer, tray, place from which to come near (e.g. waiting room), entrance';
origin_explanation: SEMITIC: Ar. qəruba 'be near', SAr. qrb, Hars. qəreb, Heb. qarab, Aram.Md.Ph.Ug. qrb, Syr. qərab, Akk. qerēbu. ETHIOPIC: Te. qäräba 'be near', Tna.Gur. qäräba, Amh. qärräba, Arg. qärräba, Har. qäräba.

meaning: 'receive Holy Communion'; 'aq'raba 'administer Holy Communion'; qərabəwi 'one who receives Holy Communion, one who has vowed not to divorce his wife'; qərban, see below.
origin_explanation: SEMITIC: Chr-Az. qarraba 'administer Holy Communion'.

meaning: 'receive Holy Communion'; Tna. qərabəa. The verb in all these languages is a denominative from qərban.
origin_explanation: ETHIOPIC: Amh. qərraḅa 'receive Holy Communion', Tna. qərabəa. The verb in all these languages is a denominative from qərban.

meaning: 'piece of cloth, garment that is not sewn'; quməsəa qərabib (Lt) 'a new roll of cloth'.
'leather bag'; probably reconstructed from Amh. qərbaḅəčča, same.

'offering, dedication, gift, sacrifice, Eucharist, oblation, Holy Communion, Host (see Hammerschmidt 1967:130-2); qərba 'receive the Eucharist'; qərba 'celebrate Mass'; 'akk'ateta qərban 'Eucharistic prayers, anaphora';
origin_explanation: SEMITIC: Ar. qurban 'Holy Eucharist', SAr. qrbn 'offering, sacrifice', Heb. qorban, Syr. qurbana, Md. qurbana. ETHIOPIC: qurban, qərban. According to Noeldeke 1910:37, Ethiopic and Arabic qurban is taken from Syriac qurbana. See also qəraba, above.

meaning: 'monkey'; from Ar. qird 'monkey'; also Amh. qərd. See also qardinat.
origin_explanation: From Ar. qird 'monkey'; also Amh. qərd.
from Ar. ğirdan 'rat' (Hommel 364).

meaning: 'lacerate, tear away, cut off, shear, shave'; 'aqarada (K*), causative; taqarada, taq'arada, pass. of qarada; qəradə, act. part. of qarada; also 'barber'; qərud 'lacerated, shorn, shaved, cut off';

origin_explanation: SEMITIC: Ar. qarada 'cut, sever, cut off'. The root is related to qaraṣa, below. For 'stomach ache' coming from the root 'cut', cp. Amh. yəhod qurtat.

meaning: 'peel off, skin, bark'; taqarafa (Lt), passive; qəraf 'bark, peel' (n); qəraft 'bark, skin, peel, rind, scale';
origin_explanation: SEMITIC: Ar. qarafa 'peel off'. ETHIOPIC: Tna. qäräfä 'peel off', Te. qärfa, qäräfä, Har. qäräfa, Gur. qärfət 'bark of tree' passed into Cushitic: Bil. qaraf, Sa. qaraf, K. qaref. The root is related to qlf; see qə'alafa, above, and qə'aräfa, below.

meaning: 'cut into little pieces, tear, from an oversize piece, (M) pick fruit or plants';
origin_explanation: SEMITIC: Ar. (Syria) qaraf 'break in two'. ETHIOPIC: Tna. qərraḅa 'eat, browse, eat the qə'arf root', Amh. qərraḅa 'bite from an oversize piece', Tna.Amh. qə'arf 'kind of potato-like root eaten by monks and ascetics'. See also qarafa, above.

meaning: 'one who has long ears'; from Amh. qə'arīfo, same.
origin_explanation: from Amh. qə'arīfo, same.

meaning: 'shave, make bald'; qərrah (K*) 'bald'; qərrah (T.M) 'shaving the head (as sign of mourning)'; qərhat 'clipping (n), shaving, tonsure, coronation' (on the occasion of coronation the hair is clipped and burned with incense);
origin_explanation: SEMITIC: Ar. qarah 'land containing neither water nor trees', Mh. qōreḥ 'shave', Heb. qarah 'make bald', Aram.-Syr. qəraḥ 'be bald'. ETHIOPIC: Amh. qərhat 'tonsure' (from Geez).

meaning: 'drain off, empty a vessel, drink to the last drop, go uninvited from house to house'; qərrah (K*), act. part. of qarrəha;

origin_explanation: ETHIOPIC: Amh. (an)qərräqərrä 'pour slowly in a thin stream' (from a reduplicated root qər? > qərr).
ETHIOPIC: Te. qärha 'take fire'; metathesis of qaha-ra, above.

meaning: 'one who rides a camel, one who guards camels';

'glean, harvest'; taqarrəma 'glean, harvest'; taqarrəma (M*) 'be gleaned'; qəram (K*), act. part. of qarama; qəramə (K*), act. part. of qarama; qərum (K*) 'gleaned, harvested'; qərum (K*) 'gleaned, harvested'; qarim, qarm 'gleanings, stubble'; qərmat (K*) 'gleaning' (n); taqrəm 'gleanings, crop, harvest'; taqarrəmə 'one who gleans or harvests';
origin_explanation: SEMITIC: Ar. qarama 'gnaw, nibble'. ETHIOPIC: Tna. qärrämä 'glean', Te. qärim 'arable land' (probably 'gleaned > arable'), Amh. qärrämä 'glean', Arg. qärräma, Gur. qərramä; passed into Cushitic: Kam. qarmə-ta 'gleanings', Som. qarmi. See also qərmay.

meaning: 'buy an ox jointly and apportion its meat among those who paid part of the price'; taqarrəmi (K*), act. part. of taqarrəma; qərmat (K*) 'act of buying an ox jointly and apportioning it';
origin_explanation: ETHIOPIC: Amh. (tä)qärämmätä 'buy an ox jointly and apportion it', Tna. qärämät 'the buying of an ox jointly and apportioning it'. The Geez form taqarrəma gives the impression of being a reconstructed form of qərmat. It is also possible to interpret the Geez form as the original form; the Tna. Amh. verbs would then be denominatives from *qərmat.

meaning: 'lush forest'; perhaps a transcription of Heb. karmel through a Greek form.
origin_explanation: perhaps a transcription of Heb. karmel through a Greek form.

meaning: 'briar, weed, oats'; to be derived from qrm I 'glean'.
origin_explanation: to be derived from qrm I 'glean'.

meaning: 'scarlet, purple, kind of resinous plant used to make torches'; qarmaZaye (Lt) 'kind of garment of purple color'
origin_explanation: from Ar. qirmiZ 'caucus (insect)', qirmiZiyy 'crimson, scarlet', from Persian of Sanskrit (krimija) ori-gin; also Amh. qarmaZ, Syr. qermeZ.
W. Müller suggests, with reservations, a loanword from Tamil kurundam 'corundum', Hindi kurand.

a rare case of q for . if it is to be compared with rnb. This would have to be considered an inverse process, since normally q becomes ', and not the other way around. For q- in Amharic, see Cohen 1939:40-1; in Gurage, see Leslau 1979:LXXXII. For qarnab in Semitic, cp. Ar. (Dat) qarnab 'hare'. The root rnb occurs in Ar. 'arnab, Mh. harneb, Syr. 'arnaba, Heb. 'arnebet, Akk. arnabu, annabu.

meaning: 'eyelash, eyelid, top of an ear of corn' (for which T has also the form q'ar-nab)
origin_explanation: ETHIOPIC: Tna. qarnab 'eyelash, eyelid', Gur. qarb, qarrab, Amh.Arg. qəndab, Gaf. qəndəba. See Praetorius 1879:78; Cohen 1931:380. The root passed into Cushitic: Kham. qarnib. See also qarb.
ETHIOPIC: perhaps Amh. qarno 'smell of a bone or horn in fire'.
identified with q'ənāmat, above, with inserted r.
from Gk. korynēn, acc. sg. of korynē 'club'.
from Gk. krēnēn, acc. sg. of krēnē 'fountain'.
ETHIOPIC: Ar. qirra, qarra 'frog', Aram. 'aqrūqta. Amh. 'aq'ərnana'at 'frog' related to Amh. 'anq'ərq'ər 'frog', Te. 'anqora', qora, Tna.
from Ar. qarawiyy 'rustic'; also Syr. qūryāyā (Dill-mann 429 has qūryānā).
variant of qalpapa I (see above). See also q'apara.

meaning: 'level and open field, (KG) boulder, heap of stones'
origin_explanation: ETHIOPIC: Amh. qarqār 'cliff'.

meaning: 'rap, knock, shout, speak noisily, be confused'; q"rq"ar 'murmuring (n), shouting (n), quarrel, argument, (T.L) commotion, agita-tion'; q"rq"aro (K*) 'shouter'; taq"arq"ar 'murmur, grumbling'
origin_explanation: SEMITIC: Ar. qarqara expresses the noise of various animals, PBh. qirqer 'cackle, cluck', Syr. qarqār 'cackle'. For qr expressing sound, cp. qr (Ur) 'call, read' (Ar.Heb), qr 'knock' (Ar). ETHIOPIC: Tna. q"ərq"ərā 'hammer', q"ərāqq"ərā 'tap idly and slowly', aqrarra 'sing songs' related to Te. qärqäha 'knock', and ('an)g"arg"ara 'murmur'. See also qāqer, above, and q"rq"r VI.

meaning: 'grieve, be uncomfortable, be angry, be upset, be in an uproar'; taq"arq"ara (K*) 'be grieving, be uncomfort-able'; q"arq"ar (K*) 'sad, grieving'; q"arq"ar (K.T) 'sorrow, anger, vexation, commotion, uproar'
origin_explanation: ETHIOPIC: Tna. q"ərq"ərā 'be sad', Te. qärqärä 'grieved', Amh. q"ərāqq"ərā 'be restless, be discontented, able', taq"āqq"ərā 'be restless, be discontented, thoughtful, regret'.
ETHIOPIC: Tna. qärqärä 'steal', and perhaps Amh. q"ərāqq"ərā 'hurt, injure'. See also k"ərk"ər I.

meaning: 'make a founda-tion, begin to settle a country, build a city'; taq"arq"ara 'be founded (a city)'
origin_explanation: SEMITIC: related to Ar. qāra (qwr) 'make a round hole', Heb. qār (qwr) 'dig'. For the relation between Heb. qwr and G. naq"ara, waqara, see Buhl 709. ETHIOPIC: Amh. q"ərāqq"ərā 'found a town or a city'.
ETHIOPIC: Tna. q"ərq"ərā 'thin', 'anq"ərq"ərā 'pour slowly in a thin stream', Amh. anq"ārāqq"ərā, Gur. aq"ərq"ərā.
for the expressions for noise, shouting, singing, see q"rq"r I.
from Amh. q"ərāqq"ərā.

meaning: 'circus, chess, the gähäta-game'; qarqisawi, qarqesawi 'player, chess player'; qarqesa (T) 'play chess' (denominative)
origin_explanation: from Gk. kirkos 'circus', extended into playing games; also PBh. qirqas, Syr. qarqesa, Aram. qirqas.

meaning: 'cold, be cool, cool down (anger), subside (fire)'; aq"rara 'cool off, cool (anger), appease, refresh'; q"rrar 'cold (n), coldness, (OT) hell, depth'; see also (nafnafa), nafnif; q"rrar (fem. q"ərrār) 'cold, cool'; q"rrar (Lt) 'cold' (n); q"rrar (Lt) 'ice'; q"rrar (adj); q"rrar (Lt) 'cold'; q"rrar (Gr.174) 'that which cools'.
'thorn'; is there any connection with qasafalu?

'be wounded, be injured, be covered with sores, be in pain'; 'aq°sala 'wound, injure, cause pain, cause a sore'; 'q°asl (pl. q°asal, q°aslāt) 'wound, sore, ulcer, pain'; 'q°asul 'wounded, injured, who is in pain'; q°aslat (K*) 'being (n) wounded'; 'aq°sālī (K*) 'one who wounds'; maq°sāl, maq°sālī 'one who wounds, one who causes pain'
origin_explanation: SEMITIC: Ar. qasala 'cut' (Dillmann 431), either from an original q-s > q-s by dissimilation, or from an original q-s > q-s by dissimilation. Praetorius 1890:23 compares G. q°sī with Ar. qasala 'shed blood, bleed (woman)'. ETHIOPIC: Tna. q°āsālā 'be wounded', Te. qāsla, Amh.Gur. q°āssālā, Arg. qusəl 'wound' (n).

meaning: qassama 'season, make tasty'; taqasama, taqassama, passive; qassami, act. part. of qassama; qassum 'well seasoned, tasty, that has savor'; qasm 'seasoning'; qassāme (K) 'being seasoned' (n); maqsam, maqsam (pl. maqāsəm) 'seasoned food'; taqsām (Gr.174) 'seasoning, condiment'
origin_explanation: SEMITIC: perhaps Ar. qasama 'choose the best dishes, eat the best dishes'. ETHIOPIC: Amh. qassama 'taste'. Praetorius 1879:95 compares this root with Amh. qāmmāmā 'taste', also Tna. qāmāsā, Te. qāmsa.

meaning: see astaqaqasama, astaqaqasama, astaqaqasama 'divine from omens, practice divination, deal in augury, foretell by means of magic'; 'aqasama (K*), causative; qasm 'divination, magic'; qasāmi, maqsam (K*) 'magician, diviner, soothsayer'; maqsam, maqsam (pl. maqāsəm, maqāsəmī) 'divination, enchantment, magic, omen, soothsaying, portents, rewards of divination, gift, present'; maqsamī, maqsamī (L') 'diviner'; maqsamī, maqsam (K*) 'diviner, soothsayer'; maqsamī, pl. maqāsəmī (K*) 'divining instrument'; maqsam (K*) 'place of divination'; mastaqāsəm 'diviner, soothsayer, (T) one who practices divination with shells'.
origin_explanation: SEMITIC: Ar. 'aqsāma 'conjure', Sar. mqs m 'oracle', Heb. qāsam 'practice divination', Aram. Asssimilation of q-s > q-s occurs in Ar. qassama 'conjure', Syr. qəṣam; dissimilation of q-s > k-s occurs in Md. kaṣma 'soothsaying', and kams (with metathesis). ETHIOPIC: Amh. qāssāmā 'foretell by means of magic'.
SEMITIC: Ar. qism 'nature', Ar. (Syria) qasm 'temperament and constitution'. ETHIOPIC: Amh. qasm 'body compounded of four elements (i.e., fire, water, air, and earth)', Te. qəsmi 'complexion'.
perhaps from Gk. kosmētērion 'dressing room'.
ETHIOPIC: Amh. q°asq°as 'household furnishings', Te. qasqas 'vessel' passed into Cushitic: Bil. qasqas.
qasqas (Lt) 'dry fire wood, brush wood, fire-brand'; ETHIOPIC: Te. qāsqāsa 'stir up, shake', Tna. qāsqāsā, qāsqāsā 'stir a fire', Amh. q°āsāqq°āsā, Gur. anqāsqāsā 'move'.
to be identified with q°asara II 'count', with > s dissimilation of glottalization.

meaning: 'be ordained a priest, perform the duties of a priest, become an elder'; 'aqasasa (K*) 'ordain to priesthood'; qasis (pl. qasīsān, qasīsāwəst) 'presbyter, priest, (Lt) elder'; (naqqa qasīs 'assistant priest'); (qasīs hasay 'father confessor'); qasus (Gr.511) 'ordained priest'; qas 'presbyter, priest'; qassannā 'priesthood, (Lt) seniority'.
origin_explanation: SEMITIC: Syr. qassīsā, qassīsā 'presbyter', Md. q-s be connected with Aram.Syr. qassīsā 'old, elder', Md. qasīs(a); Ar. qissīs, qassīs 'priest, presbyter', Synac loanword (Fraenkel 275). ETHIOPIC: Tna. qāsīsī 'priest', Te. qās, qāsī, Amh.Har.Gur. qes; Tna. qāsāsā 'be ordained priest', Amh. qāsāsā. G. qasīs is considered a Synac loanword (Noeldeke 1910:37, Jeffery while G. qas, Tna. qāsīsī are forms developed within Ethiopic. See also qays.

meaning: 'bow', name of the index finger and of the middle finger in a guessing game (Dillmann 1357, under fss); (qasta dammanā, qasta 'əgzī'abəhēr 'rainbow'); qastām, qastam 'bow, shepherd's crook, knotty club, rod, stick'; qastawa, see below
origin_explanation: SEMITIC: Ar. qaws 'bow', Heb. qešet, Aram. qastā, Jew.-Aram. qustā (with q-t > q-t), Syr. qeštā 'bow', qāštā 'archer', Ug. qšt 'bow', Md. qašta, Ph. Akk. qaštu. For more details on this root, see Noeldeke 1910:132-3. ETHIOPIC: Tna. qāstī 'bow', Amh.Gur. qāst, Te. qast (with inserted r). Amh. qāstam 'short club, cudgel' passed into Cushitic: Bil. qist, probably Som. qanso. For 'rainbow' expressed by 'bow of the clouds', cp. Tna. qāsta dāmmānā, Amh. qāstā dammānā, Te. qas dābana, Gaf. qāstā dāmmānā; also Ar. qawsu s-sahāb. For the expression of 'rainbow' in the Hamito-Semitic (Afroasiatic) languages, see Orbis 5 (1956), 478-483.

meaning: 'make a bow, bend a bow, shoot with an arrow, (Y) render tight (a bow) by pulling, pull, hit, throw'
origin_explanation: qastāwī (K*), act. part. of qastawa; denominative of qast (see above); also Tna. qās(s)ātā 'bend a bow'.

either directly from Gk. kostos 'a root used as a spice', or through Ar. qust, kust; also SAt. qst, Syr. qūstā, qūstā, qūstā, qūstā, qūstā 'costus' from Ing from Sanskrit kustha, see Littmann 1926:415. Note the various forms with q-k, t-t through dissimilation or assimilation.

meaning: 'big stomach of ruminants, (M.T) muscle of the abdomen, cut of meat from the ventral region, muscle of the flank'
origin_explanation: ETHIOPIC: Tna. q°āstē 'big stomach of the ruminants', Amh. q°āstī, Te. qās. If the original root is qst, the final t became t in Amharic by dissimilation; in Te. qās, the t is omitted as in qās 'bow' from qāst, qāst. See also qosāyā.
SEMITIC: Ar. quttā', qittā, PbH. qiššū, Aram. qattayyā, Syr. qattūṭā, Akk. qiššū. ETHIOPIC: Amh. q°asa 'cucumber', qusayat 'pumpkin'. See also qatāy, qosāyāt.
is there any connection with q°aste?
see also qodāyā.
OT describes it as 'red flower that resembles the q°asa-cucumber'; see q°əsāy, above.

meaning: one who harvests, grape gatherer; qaššām 'harvest, vintage, picking'; ('aqšām, pl. of qaššām 'harvested crops, things plucked') qaššāmāt 'picking (n), harvesting' (n); qaššāmē 'that which is harvested or picked'; maqaššām (pl. maqaššām) 'vintage, instrument for harvesting'; taqšām 'gathering (n), harvest (of fruit)'
origin_explanation: SEMITIC: SAr qašm 'vegetable plot', Ar. (Yemen) maqaššama (W. Müller). ETHIOPIC: Tna. qāššāmā 'gather nectar', Amh. qāššāmā.

qaššār 'fish scales, shell'; qeššār (T); rather qaššār (?) 'piece, split'; SEMITIC: Ar. qišr 'rind, fish scales'. ETHIOPIC: Amh. qaššār 'scales (of fish)' is probably taken from Geez, while the Amharic form is qəršāt (with metathesis for qašrāt). Har. qašīr 'coffee made of the beans' peel and seasoned with ginger'. Gur. qašər 'coffee bean without its peel' are borrowed from Ar. (Yemen) qišr 'coffee made of the peel and seasoned with spices'.

meaning: vase, water pot, container for mead'
origin_explanation: SEMITIC: Ar. qašwa 'small basket', Ar. (Oman) qašš 'utensil' (Vollers, ZDMG 49[1895].508), Aram. qāšūṭā, Ug. qš. Fraenkel 63-4 (following Noeldeke) compares Heb. qešeq 'writing case'. Syr. qešqā 'pot'. However, Heb. qešeq is considered to be borrowed from Egyptian qšty 'tablet of writer' (M. Müller, OLZ 3[1900].49, T. Lambdin, JAOS 53[1973].194). For Syr. qešqā, it is taken from Gk. Kseštes 'pitcher' according to Brockelmann 1928:679, Grimme, OLZ 3 (1900). 150 identifies G. qašūṭ with G. qʻaṣqʻas 'vessel'. Reinisch 1902:248 compares G. qašūṭ with Som. kūsad 'water pot'.

meaning: steal, rob, carry off, take away by fraud, defraud, embezzle'

origin_explanation: Akk. kašāṭu 'cut off'. ETHIOPIC: Tna. qāššāṭā 'hide the truth, lie, cheat', Amh. qāššāṭā 'steal, dupe'.

meaning: humiliate, subject, bend, make fall to the ground, overthrow, punish, cut out, level to ground, sweep, pave, trample on, crush'; qaṣa'a, causative; also 'humiliate, bring low'; taqaṣa'a, pass. of qaṣa'a; also 'be pressed down, be overcome with awe, stoop, bow down, be (intr), fall in ruins, collapse, faint, sink to the ground, be overwhelmed with terror, (T) 'chopped up'
origin_explanation: SEMITIC: Ar. qaṣa'a 'humiliate', qada'a 'subjugate', taqada'a 'be cut into small pieces', Ar. (Hadr) qaṣ' 'cut', SAr. qṣ', Šh. qeda' 'gnaw through', Heb. qaṣaṣ (hifil) 'scrape off'. Syr. qaṣṣa', Ug. qṣ-t 'crusher, bruiser'. ETHIOPIC: Tna. qāṣṣe 'punish', Te. qāṣa'a, Amh.Arg. Gur. qāṭṭa. Har. qatawa; passed into Cushitic: Bil. qate 'punish', Sa. qāṣa'a 'subjugate'. The root is related to qʻi', qada'a, qadʻa.

meaning: nod, beckon, wink, make a sign, give a signal, hint, indicate, entice, mock (by winking or gesture), deride, speak in parables'; qaṣṣaba 'deny contemptuously by a gesture'; taqaṣṣaba, taqaṣṣabā 'mock by winking or gestures, make faces at someone, point a finger (in scorn), ridicule'; taqaṣṣabā 'wink, wink at one another, nod with the eyes, mock one another by gestures, despise, scoff, ridicule'; qaṣāb 'nod'; qaṣābāt 'wink, twinkling, beckoning, nod, (K*) 'proverb (used for hinting), enigma'
origin_explanation: (kama qaṣabāt 'in a moment'); (kama qaṣabātā 'ayn 'in the twinkling of an eye, in a flash, in a split second'); taqaṣṣābī (M) 'one who winks, one who mocks'; taqaṣṣabo, taqaṣṣabot (Li) 'mockery, derision'. ETHIOPIC: Te. qāṣba, qāṣba'a 'twinkle, beckon with the hands', Tna. qāṣābā, Amh. qāṭṭabā 'wink', taqāṭṭabābā 'make fun of'; Te.Tna. qāṣbāt 'wink'. Amh. qaṣṣābāt 'wink, twinkling', kāmā qaṣbāt 'in a flash, in a moment', is taken from Geez. The root passed into Cushitic: Bil. qʻaṣāb 'twinkle'.
metathesis in relation to qabṣa.
ETHIOPIC: Amh. qāṣāfālu, same, from Geez. See also qaṣafalu.

meaning: have something placed on the head (e.g. headpiece, wreath, crown), be crowned, crown oneself, wear a crown'; 'aqqaṣṣala (K*) 'crown' (v); 'astaqqaṣṣala 'put a crown on the head, crown'; qaṣṣāla 'crown, diadem, garland, wreath, wreathed border, rim, fringe, border, silk head covering fringed in gold with which priests cover their heads during Mass, (T) helmet'; qaṣṣul 'crowned'
origin_explanation: SEMITIC: J. Assfalg, OC 38 (1954).139-9 derives G. qaṣṣala from Ar. qaṣla < Syr. quṣṣālā, quṣṣālē < Gk. kasoula 'hood' (of Latin [casula] origin). Dillmann 472 wrongly combines it with G. qʻaṣāla 'put forth leaves'. Note that Brockelmann 1928:680 derives Syr. quṣṣālā from Gk. kasoula, but does not compare it with Arabic or Geez. Praetorius 1890:24 suggests, with reservations, the derivation of qaṣṣala from Ar. qalaṣuwa 'headgear'. ETHIOPIC: Tna. qāṣṣala 'hanging end of turban', Amh. qāṣṣāla 'silk head covering', from Geez.

meaning: add, join, continue'; 'aqqaṣṣala (K*), causative; taqaṣṣala (K*), pass. of qaṣṣala; qaṣṣul (K*) 'added, joined'; qaṣṣāl (K*) 'something attached to a thing, adjective';

origin_explanation: ETHIOPIC: Tna.Amh. qäsäla 'slab, tablet'; perhaps also Te. qäsäla 'small thin stones between hearth and kettle'. For 'plate of metal', see also q°äsəl, below, under q°asäla.

meaning: 'put forth leaves, be verdant'; 'aq°sala 'put forth leaves, be verdant'; 'aq°səl (pl. q°əslät, 'aq°səl) 'leaf, foliage, leaf of a book, metal plate (by extension)'; q°əssäl 'covered with foliage'; q°əsəl, q°usul 'full of foliage, covered with foliage, leafy, bushy (tree)'.
origin_explanation: SEMITIC: the connection with Ar. qasala 'cut', qasil 'fourrage vert coupé pour les chevaux' suggested by Appleyard 1977:40, and by Rabin HS:91 who compares Ar. qasala 'give fodder' is doubtful, the basic meaning of the Arabic root being 'cut'. Likewise, the connection with Ar. hūṣ 'palm leaves' suggested by Cohen 1941:no. 159 (who gives hawṣ), with reservations, is doubtful. The root is perhaps related to Ar. hdl (x) 'have many branches and leaves', as suggested by Dillmann 472. ETHIOPIC: Tna. q°äsli, Te. qätäl, Amh. qätäl, Arg. qätal, Har. quttii, Gur. qätäl, qäte. Reinisch 1884:29 compares Kham. xaṣä, Agau xasä.
perhaps connected with qasimat, below.
ETHIOPIC: perhaps Amh. qätäma 'rush, reed'.
perhaps a misprint for qappäp̣e, above. Getatchew thinks that qasäp̣e means 'scorpion' and is to be compared with šaqamp̣e 'scorpion' (see below), with metathesis.

meaning: 'be agonized by pain or trembling, (T.Y) munch, crush, crunch, be affected with convulsion, agitate, make tremble'

origin_explanation: SEMITIC: reduplicated root qss or qsy: Ar. qasqasa 'cut with scissors'. For qss, qsy, see qasaya, below. The root qsqs is related to qtqt I, below.

meaning: 'enclose with a wall, blockade, hedge in, surround, delimit, fortify'; 'aq°sara (K*), causative; taqäsara (K*), passive of qasara; qasäri (K*), act. part. of qasara; qasur, pass. part. of qasara; also 'besieged'; qasr, qasr (pl. 'aq°sär) 'wall, fence, hedge, sure, citadel, stronghold, fortress, fortification, rampart, castle'
origin_explanation: SEMITIC: Ar. qasara 'lock up', Aram. (Nabatacan) qsr 'cell'. ETHIOPIC: Tna. qäsärä 'enclose', Te. 'aq°sära 'preserve, guard', Amh. qattärä 'enclose', Amh.Gur. qätär 'enclosure'. G. qasr is taken from Arabic qasr, considered to come from Latin castra. The root passed into Cushitic: Kham. qiser 'enclosure', Qua. qeter.

meaning: 'bind up, bind together, tie up, knot, tie in a knot, enclose, (Gr.424, Y) ensnare, contrive, conspire'; (with gass 'face' it means 'make the face sad, contract the face austerely, have a gloomy or sad look, frown, wrinkle'); q°əssara 'bind together, gather'; q°äsära 'tie, bind together, bring together'; 'aq°sara, caus. of q°äsara; also 'incite to evil'; taq°äsara, pass. of q°äsara; 'aq°äsära 'be united with one another, conspire against one another, lay an ambush for one another'; 'astaq°äsara, caus. of taq°äsara; q°äsäri (K*), act. part. of q°äsara; q°əsur, pass. part. of q°äsara; also 'constricted, furrowed (with grief)'; q°äsura gass 'ill-tempered, sullen, one with an austere look'); q°əsur 'his face is gloomy'); q°äsär 'band, knot, constraint, fraud, deceit, deception'.
origin_explanation: SEMITIC: Ar. qsr 'bring in harvest' (W. Müller, ZAW 75[1963]:314), Heb. qāṣar 'reap, harvest' (from the meaning 'tie the sheaves'), PBh. qṣr 'bind', Akk. kaṣāru (by dissimilation); related to qtr. Ar. qatara 'tie the halters of camels, dispose them in a file', Syr. qəṭar 'tie, bind', Aram. qṭr. Md. qṭar. Note that Brockelmann 1908:154 derives Aram. qṭr from *qtr > Heb. qṣr > Aram. qṭr (from qṭr, by assimilation); so also Dillmann 473. For a possible connection with Heb. qāšar 'bind', see also Leslau 1969:65. The connection between G. q°äsara and Heb. qšr 'be short' suggested by Fraenkel, BA 3(1898).63 is unlikely. ETHIOPIC: Tna. q°äsärä 'knot', Amh. q°attärä, q°attära, Gur. qätärä, Har. qatära. For 'frown, wrinkle' with 'face', cp. also Amh. fit-un q°attärä 'frown', lit. 'tie the face'. The root passed into Cushitic: Bil. q°äsär 'tie, knot', Kham. q°asär, Sa. q°asar.

meaning: 'reckon'; 'aq°sara (K*), causative; taq°äsara (K*), passive; q°äsäri (K*), act. part. of q°äsara; q°əsər (K*) 'count'; maq°särt, pl.maq°äsər (T.Gr.180) 'rosary, incantation, charm'.
origin_explanation: ETHIOPIC: Tna. q°äsärä 'count', Amh.Gur. q°ättärä, Arg. q°ättära, Gaf. q°ittärä; passed into Cushitic: Kham. q°aser 'count'. For G. maq°särt, cp. Tna. maq°säriya 'rosary', Amh. maq°täriya. See also qasara, above.
SEMITIC: Ar. qasā (qsw) 'mutilate', Soq. qeṣ (qṣṣ)

meaning: 'network, fringe, interlaced thing'; q°asrat 'tying (n), binding, joining, ligament, setting of a ring or gem, texture (that is, intertwining), conception'; q°asratä (Gr.455) 'conception by the V. Mary', lit. 'her conception', (originally holding the fruit as a knot in her womb); maq°särt 'setting (of a ring or gem)'; taq°äsära, part. of taq°äsara.
origin_explanation: SEMITIC: SAr. qsr 'bring in harvest' (W. Müller, ZAW 75[1963]:314), Heb. qāṣar 'reap, harvest' (from the meaning 'tie the sheaves'), PBh. qṣr 'bind', Akk. kaṣāru (by dissimilation); related to qtr. Ar. qatara 'tie the halters of camels, dispose them in a file', Syr. qəṭar 'tie, bind', Aram. qṭr. Md. qṭar. Note that Brockelmann 1908:154 derives Aram. qṭr from *qtr > Heb. qṣr > Aram. qṭr (from qṭr, by assimilation); so also Dillmann 473. For a possible connection with Heb. qāšar 'bind', see also Leslau 1969:65. The connection between G. q°äsara and Heb. qšr 'be short' suggested by Fraenkel, BA 3(1898).63 is unlikely. ETHIOPIC: Tna. q°äsärä 'knot', Amh. q°attärä, q°attära, Gur. qätärä, Har. qatära. For 'frown, wrinkle' with 'face', cp. also Amh. fit-un q°attärä 'frown', lit. 'tie the face'. The root passed into Cushitic: Bil. q°äsär 'tie, knot', Kham. q°asär, Sa. q°asar.

meaning: 'cut', Heb. qāṣā (qṣw/y) 'cut off', Syr. qəṣā 'break (bread)', Ph. qṣw; related to qṣṣ, qwd. Ar. qaṣṣa 'cut off', qasqasa 'cut with scissors', qāda (qwd) 'split', Heb. qāṣaṣ (qṣṣ) 'tear asunder', qāṣaṣ 'cut off', Syr. qṣaṣ (qṣṣ), Md.Ug. qṣṣ, Akk. kaṣāṣu, qaṣāṣu 'prune off, kāṣu 'strip off'.
origin_explanation: ETHIOPIC: Tna. qäṣäyä, qäṣäyä, qäṣäw bälä 'cut', Amh. qäčä, Har. qäča. The root is common with Cushitic: Kam. qočče 'hit, castrate', Mo. qočči (ye) 'scratch'. On the root in Cushitic, see Cerulli 1938a:82, under qad. See also qasqasa.
from Ar. qittā, quttā, with the Arabic article al-. Also Amh. quṭl. See also q°ässay, maqiṭä.

meaning: 'kill, put to death, slay, murder, execute, slaughter, attack, engage in battle, combat, fight, wage war'; (zayəqaṭtəl 'deadly'); 'aqtala, causative; also 'cause to be killed'; taqätala, passive of qatala; taqätala 'kill one another, fight with one another, fight together, make war'; 'astaqätala, caus. of taqätala; also 'help to kill, incite to kill'; qatāli, act. part. of qatala; also 'killer, murderer, assassin, fatal, deadly'; qətul, passive part. of qatala; also 'attacked (city)'; qatl, qätl 'murder, killing, slaughter, massacre, battle, combat, fighting, war, strife'; qatili 'killing, execution'; qätlat 'murder, killing, slaughter, massacre, assault, attack, execution'; maqtal (pl. maqätali) 'murderer, enemy, opponent'; maqtäl, maqtäl, pl. maqätält (K*) 'means of killing'; maqtäl 'murderer'; maqtäl 'place of killing'; maqtalät 'warrior, combatant, fighter'; mäqtalät (K*) 'place of killing'; taqätalot (Lt) 'warfare, attack'.
origin_explanation: SEMITIC: Ar. qatala 'kill', Heb. qāṭal (from Aramaic qṭl), ancient Aram. qṭl (Jean-Hoftijzer 257), Syr. qəṭal, Akk. qatālu (from Canaanite). On Soq. laṭah 'kill', Mh. letog, see Leslau 1938:226. See on this root Wagner 100-1.

meaning: 'kill', 'to kill'. Te. qatla. Praetorius 1879:72 connects Amh. qäddalä 'kill' with qtl; it is rather to be connected with G. gadala 'dominate in fight', Heb. gdl 'be strong'.
origin_explanation: Te. qatla. Praetorius 1879:72 connects Amh. qäddalä 'kill' with qtl; it is rather to be connected with G. gadala 'dominate in fight', Heb. gdl 'be strong'.

meaning: 'fasten with nails, shut up, seal, lock, bolt, enclose with a fence, (K*) sharpen', 'toqattara, taqattara, passive', 'qattar 'nailed down, locked, closed, sealed'; qatr (pl. qatrāt) 'peg, nail, spike, goad, spur, harrow, stick'', 'qatrāt, verbal noun of qtr'.
origin_explanation: SEMITIC: For qatrāt 'sticks, spurs'. Dillmann 442 compares Ar. qatr 'javelin', qatīr 'heads of nails in a coat of mail'. ETHIOPIC: Te. qātra 'enclose'. Tna. qātärä 'fix, place firmly'. Amh. qātärä 'lock a door tightly, fasten with nails', qatārī 'spike'; passed into Cushitic: Bil. qater 'enclose'.

meaning: (Y) qattara 'fumigate, (T) give off an odor'; qattārē, (K*) qättār 'incense, fumigation, odoriferous substance'.
origin_explanation: SEMITIC: Ar. qatūr 'incense', miqtar 'censer', Sar. m-qtr 'incense altar', Heb. qəṭōreṭ 'incense', Aram. 'aqṭer 'let incense rise', Md. qtr 'fumigate, cense', Ug. qtr 'smoke, incense'. It also occurs as qtr by dissimilation of the emphatic consonant: Ar. qatara 'exhale an odor (cooked meat)', qattara 'fumigate the body or the clothes with camel's dung in order that wild animals may not perceive his (the hunter's) smell'. Akk. qatāru 'fumigate, make an incense offering'.
ETHIOPIC: Te. qātra 'be noontide'. Tna. qātärä, Amh. qātärä 'be noontide', qātr 'noon heat'.

meaning: (O. Neugebauer, personal communication) 'lunar (date)'; from Gk. kata selenēn 'according to the moon (i.e., lunar)'.
origin_explanation: from Gk. kata selenēn 'according to the moon (i.e., lunar)'.

meaning: (T.Y.M) 'make a wager, bet'; qatat 'agreement, contract, stipulation, treaty'.
